tions.

Projects
OR-Formatter Application July 2016 - December 2017
Environment: Java, MySQL, Shell Script, Jenkins.•Developed an application to process Geneva Modified File (GMF) from Geneva server, generating Bill backup and
Bill Invoices for client customers of BT.
•Applied change requests to current modules and contributed to requirement analysis and development for user
stories.
•Performed thorough dev unit testing for user stories and effectively handled E2E issues and bug fixes.
•Prepared necessary program-level and user-level documentation for the application.
STAA Middleware Layer January 2018 - March 2018
Environment: Java EJB, MySQL, Shell Script, Weblogic12c Server, Jenkins.•Developed STAA, an encapsulation middleware layer acting as an interface between client’s operational support
systems and Legacy systems.
•Transformed legacy data into various formats such as TLV; input received in XML/HTTP format, with validations
performed before sending to backend systems (CSS and COSMOSS Databases).
•Created services using Java/J2EE and contributed to requirement analysis, development, and dev unit testing for
user stories.
•Handled E2E issues, performed bug fixing, and successfully managed migration from Java 1.6 to Java 1.8.
Skills
Programming Languages: Java/J2EE, Swift, HTML, Shell Script
Databases: MySQL
Tools & Technologies: SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, Weblogic12c, Android
IDEs & Software: Android Studio, Eclipse, Oracle, Xcode
Operating Systems: Windows 10, Mac OS (High Sierra)

Ibrahim Sayed is a Java Developer with contact information including email (ibrahim.sayed@email.com) and a GitHub profile (github.com/ibrahim-sayed). He also has a LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/ibrahim-sayed) and a personal website (ibrahimsayed.com).

Ibrahim Sayed received a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology in June 2016, located in Mumbai, India, and a Diploma in Information Technology from Thakur Polytechnic in May 2013, also in Mumbai, India.

From July 2016 to March 2018, Ibrahim Sayed worked as a Java Developer at Tech Mahindra in Mumbai, India, where he applied change requests, developed new features, conducted requirement analysis, performed unit testing, handled issues, and contributed to Java version migration from 1.6 to 1.8.

From July 2016 to December 2017, Ibrahim Sayed developed the OR-Formatter Application using Java, MySQL, Shell Script, and Jenkins, which processed Geneva Modified Files (GMF) for BT customers, generating Bill backups and invoices. He also performed requirement analysis, unit testing, and documentation.

From January 2018 to March 2018, Ibrahim Sayed developed STAA Middleware Layer using Java EJB, MySQL, Shell Script, Weblogic12c Server, and Jenkins, which acted as an interface between client’s operational support systems and Legacy systems, transforming data into formats like TLV.

Ibrahim Sayed possesses skills in programming languages like Java/J2EE, Swift, HTML, and Shell Script, databases like MySQL, tools and technologies like SVN, Jenkins, Hudson, Weblogic12c, Android, and IDEs/Software like Android Studio, Eclipse, Oracle, and Xcode, and operating systems like Windows 10 and Mac OS (High Sierra).

Ibrahim Sayed is a resourceful Java Developer, forged in the tech landscape of Mumbai, India. Educated at Thakur Polytechnic and later earning a Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology from Vidyalankar Institute of Technology, he quickly transitioned into practical application at Tech Mahindra, where he spent nearly two years honing his skills. There, he wasn’t simply maintaining code; he was actively involved in the evolution of systems, skillfully applying change requests, analyzing requirements, and even steering a crucial migration from Java 1.6 to the more modern 1.8. 

Beyond his core role, Ibrahim is a builder. He architected the OR-Formatter Application, a Java-based solution leveraging MySQL, Shell Script, and Jenkins to process critical billing data for BT customers – a testament to his ability to translate complex needs into functional software. He further demonstrated this aptitude with the STAA Middleware Layer, a sophisticated interface bridging legacy systems with modern operational support, expertly utilizing Java EJB, Weblogic12c, and data transformation techniques like TLV. 

His toolkit is broad and adaptable, encompassing proficiency in Java/J2EE, Swift, HTML, and Shell Scripting, alongside database expertise in MySQL. He’s equally comfortable navigating development environments like Eclipse, Android Studio, and Xcode, and deploying solutions on platforms like Weblogic12c and Jenkins. A pragmatic developer, Ibrahim is fluent in both Windows 10 and Mac OS (High Sierra), and maintains an active online presence through his GitHub profile (github.com/ibrahim-sayed), LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/ibrahim-sayed), and personal website (ibrahimsayed.com). He can be reached at ibrahim.sayed@email.com.

Mumbai is a city of relentless energy, a sprawling metropolis on India’s western coast that relentlessly churns out ambition and opportunity. It’s a place where dreams are forged in the heat and humidity, and where the pursuit of a future often begins within the walls of institutions like Vidyalankar Institute of Technology and Thakur Polytechnic – both cradles of technical skill within the city’s bustling landscape. 

Mumbai isn’t merely a location; it’s a proving ground. For individuals like Ibrahim Sayed, it’s where foundational education in Information Technology takes root, blossoming first at Thakur Polytechnic, then maturing at Vidyalankar. It’s a city that doesn’t just *house* careers, it *launches* them. 

Sayed’s trajectory exemplifies this. Emerging from Vidyalankar in 2016, he immediately stepped into the demanding world of Tech Mahindra, another significant node in Mumbai’s tech ecosystem. There, amidst the city’s constant motion, he honed his skills as a Java Developer, navigating the complexities of software development – from implementing changes and analyzing requirements to rigorous testing and even contributing to crucial system upgrades. 

Mumbai, in this context, is the backdrop against which technical expertise is refined, where young professionals like Sayed translate academic knowledge into practical application, and where the pulse of India’s technological advancement beats strongest. It’s a city that demands resilience, rewards dedication, and consistently offers a stage for those ready to build their future.
